tasks.title: Expand Arrival Assessment into a unified Tutorial+Assessment quest
tasks.description: The OnboardingManager defines a 10-step sequence (arrival cinematic, movement tutorial, reading assessment, chat UI, writing assessment, interaction tutorial, listening assessment, quest log UI, conversation assessment, results). However, it's a simple step-by-step flow with no contextual guidance. Expand it into a proper tutorial quest. (1) Add a 'tutorial' questType value to the quest system. Tutorial quests have special properties: persistent HUD hints, highlighted UI elements, forced camera angles, and cannot be abandoned. (2) Rewrite the onboarding flow as a tutorial quest with objectives that teach controls: 'Walk to the town square' (teaches WASD), 'Look around' (teaches mouse look), 'Talk to the welcome NPC' (teaches E to interact), 'Open your inventory' (teaches I key), 'Read the notice board' (teaches reading mechanics), 'Open your quest log' (teaches J key). (3) Interleave assessment phases between tutorial objectives: after movement tutorial → reading assessment, after interaction tutorial → listening assessment, after first NPC conversation → conversation assessment. (4) The tutorial quest should start with the game intro cutscene (from the narrative system) establishing the story premise. (5) Add tooltip-style HUD overlays that highlight the relevant UI element during each tutorial step (e.g., golden outline around the minimap when teaching 'press M for map'). (6) Track tutorial completion per-playthrough so it only runs on the first playthrough.

tasks.title: Create contextual control hints that appear during gameplay
tasks.description: Beyond the initial tutorial, players need contextual hints when they encounter new mechanics for the first time. (1) Create shared/game-engine/rendering/ContextualHintSystem.ts that shows brief, non-intrusive hint popups (bottom of screen, auto-dismiss after 5 seconds) when the player encounters a mechanic for the first time. (2) Track which hints have been shown in the save state (Set of hint IDs). (3) Hints to implement: first NPC approach ('Press E to talk'), first building entrance ('Press E near door to enter'), first item on ground ('Press E to pick up'), first notice board ('Press E to read notices'), first quest received ('Press J to open quest log'), first shop NPC ('You can buy and sell items here'), first combat encounter ('Click to attack, Space to dodge'), first text document found ('Press E to read — answer questions for XP'), first minimap view ('Press M for full map'), first menu open ('Press Escape for menu, save, and journal'). (4) Hints should respect the player's language — show in both the target language and English for beginners. (5) Never show the same hint twice per playthrough. (6) Wire hints to the GameEventBus events that indicate the player is near the relevant mechanic.

tasks.title: Build game intro sequence with story setup
tasks.description: The game currently drops the player into the world with no context. Build an intro sequence. (1) When a new playthrough starts, before the player can move, show a multi-page narrative intro using the NarrativeCutscenePanel (from the narrative task list). (2) The intro should establish: where the player is (the settlement name and country), why they're here (language immersion program / cultural exchange), the inciting incident (news of the missing writer), and the player's goal (explore, learn the language, solve the mystery). (3) The intro text should be generated from the world's narrative data (settlement name, target language, writer name). (4) After the intro, transition smoothly into the tutorial quest. (5) The intro is skippable for returning players (show 'Skip Intro' button). (6) Store intro-shown state in the playthrough so it doesn't replay on load.

tasks.title: Add CEFR level selection screen for players who skip assessment
tasks.description: Not all players want to sit through a full language assessment. Provide an alternative. (1) Before the tutorial/assessment begins, show a screen: 'Would you like to take a placement test (recommended) or select your level?' (2) If the player chooses to select their level, show 4 options with descriptions: A1 (Complete Beginner — 'I know almost no [French]'), A2 (Elementary — 'I know basic words and simple phrases'), B1 (Intermediate — 'I can handle everyday conversations'), B2 (Upper Intermediate — 'I can discuss complex topics'). (3) Selecting a level skips the 4 assessment phases but still runs the tutorial objectives (movement, interaction, UI). (4) The selected CEFR level is stored in the playthrough and used for all adaptive systems. (5) Add a 'Retake Assessment' option in the Game Menu settings that lets players redo the assessment at any time to update their level.
